title: GetHashCode should not reference mutable fields
description: "GetHashCode" should not reference mutable fieldsAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugQuick FixIDE quick fixes available with SonarQube for IDEWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?GetHashCodeis used to file an object in aDictionaryorHashtable. IfGetHashCodeuses non-readonlyfields and those fields change after the object is stored, the object immediately becomes mis-filed in theHashtable. Any subsequent test to see if the object is in theHashtablewill return a false negative.ExceptionsThis rule does not raise if the type implementingGetHashCodeis a value type, for example astructor arecord struct, since when a value type is stored in aDictionaryorHashtable, a copy of the value is stored, not a reference to the value.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect iss
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects GetHashCode override methods (candidates for mutable field usage verification)
logic.triggering_conditions: Identifies GetHashCode implementations for manual review of field mutability
logic.exclusion_conditions: Excludes: struct/record struct types (value types), readonly fields, comments
logic.reasoning: This rule requires semantic analysis to: 1) Identify fields accessed in GetHashCode, 2) Determine if those fields are mutable (not readonly, not const), 3) Exclude value types. Without semantic analysis, we can only detect GetHashCode method declarations as candidates. Proper implementation needs: symbol resolution to find field references, type analysis to check readonly/const modifiers, and type system knowledge to exclude structs. This heuristic flags GetHashCode methods containing property/field access patterns for manual review.

should_trigger: public override int GetHashCode() { return Name.GetHashCode(); }
public override int GetHashCode() { return this.mutableField.GetHashCode(); }
should_not_trigger: public override int GetHashCode() { return readonlyName.GetHashCode(); }
// struct Person { public override int GetHashCode() { return Name.GetHashCode(); } }
